narrative: The suspect is a tall male wearing a black hooded sweat shirt with a blue backpack. No further description was provided. I responded to the listed location on a report of a possible burglary to auto. Upon arrival, I spoke to the complainant who stated he was inside his residence and saw his vehicle interior light on. The complainant then stated he approached his front door and turned on his porch light. Once the light was turned on the suspect fled the scene and ran North on Maple Hill Drive. There was no sign of forced entry to the vehicle due to the complainant stating it was left unlocked. The complainant also stated nothing was taken from the vehicle. A New Jersey Victim Witness Notification form was completed and the complainant was provided a copy. The vehicle was unable to be processed due to it being contaminated by the complainant prior to my arrival. It was later realized that the suspect matched the description of the suspect that was involved in another burglary to auto (Reference Case #25-064712).

incident_types: Burglary - Auto, Criminal Mischief - Vehicle, Suspicious Person
narrative: Suspect description: Black male, 5' 10" - 6' in height, large build, black hooded sweatshirt, black pants, blue drawstring backpack P.O. Francavilla and I responded to the listed location on a burglary of an auto. Upon arrival, we met with the victim, who reported that her husband saw the suspect run out the driveway after the lights on the vehicle began flashing. The victim reported that her vehicle captured the incident on Tesla's onboard cameras. The video shows the suspect walking down to the rear of the driveway, walking onto their patio, and grabbing a box of tiles. After he grabbed the box of tiles, he walked over to the listed vehicle and began breaking the front passenger window with the box of tiles. The suspect then reached in and grabbed the victim's purse and fled on foot up the out of the driveway in an unknown direction. Sgt/Det. Seretis, Det. Carrillo, and Det. Reo responded to the area and located the victim's purse in front of 355 Summit Avenue. The purse and items left on the ground could not be processed because they had been left in the rain. A supervisor was requested, and Sgt. Briggs responded and was advised of the situation The purse and items were returned to the victim who reported that nothing was missing from her purse. The victim was advised to send the video footage to my departmental E-mail once she is able to upload it from the vehicle. The vehicle was not processed due to the weather and the suspect on video not touching any of the surfaces of the vehicle. While we were speaking to the victim, we located a pair of eye glasses that were outside the passenger door. The victim stated the glasses did not belong to her. It is unclear if the eye glasses belong to the suspect or another victim. While on scene, there was another burglary of an auto at 405 Central Avenue. See case number 25-064713 for further information. The glasses were secured and placed into evidence in a brown paper bag and placed into locker # 2. The cost to replace the passenger side window is approximately $500.00. The vehicle policy is listed below. A New Jersey victim notification form was completed. USAA Insurance company Policy number: 018170314C Company code: 893
